Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
G. Martin Velez, Esq. 415-342-4125 for Petitioner.
Brian Fong, Esq. 415-774-2977 of Sheppard Mullin for Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MONICA GAYTAN's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Petitioner Monica Gaytan's motion to confirm arbitration award and for entry of judgment is granted.

A court may vacate an arbitrator's award only on a narrow set of grounds. (CCP 1286.2; Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 12-13.) As the California Supreme Court teaches: "The arbitrator's decision should be the end, not the beginning, of the dispute." (Id. at 10.)

Here, defendant concedes the arbitrator was authorized to award attorney fees, but complains about the amount, claiming the arbitrator "exceeded her powers." (See CCP 1286.2(a)(4).) However, defendant cites no case involving private litigants in which an arbitral award has ever been vacated because the amount of attorney fees was deemed unreasonable. Even were it lawful (it isn't), I would not presume to substitute my judgment for that of the arbitrator who adjudicated the case. Having courts fly-speck arbitral attorney fee awards as defendant advocates could only lead to the never-ending litigation our supreme court warned against. (Moncharsh, 3 Cal.4th at 10-11.)

A proposed order is submitted by email. Movant to prepare a Proposed Judgment approved as to form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment
